op1_structure.detail: 30 labels/page (calendar-like), not 7 (Tacuinum). Two types: dense prose + short labels.

op4_galenic.detail: chi2=0.0007 looks great BUT same result in ALL sections (herbal=0.0006, pharma=0.0005). Permutation p=1.0. Artifact of Zipf distribution.

op6_crossref.detail: 41% of astro vocab shared with herbal. Same codebook across sections. Top shared: daiin, chol, aiin, dar, dal.

op7_repetitivity.detail: Astro TTR=0.452 (highest of all sections). Labels TTR=0.72 (unique names). Dense TTR=0.45 (diverse prose). NOT repetitive like Tacuinum columns.

op5_positional.detail: CV=0.021-0.027 for all K values. Uniform vocabulary at all positions. No fixed-column signature. This is PROSE.

overall_verdict.summary: The VMS astro/cosmo section is NOT a Tacuinum Sanitatis. It is prose with unique labels (like a calendar or zodiac commentary), sharing 41% vocabulary with the herbal section. The galenic ratio match was a false positive (artifact of Zipf distribution).
overall_verdict.what_it_IS: Most likely a zodiac/calendar text with labels near figures (nymphs) and prose commentary. The labels (TTR=0.72) are unique names/descriptions. The dense text is diverse prose, not fixed tabular entries.
still_useful: The Tacuinum substance list (280 entries) is still useful for R02_ingredients enrichment
The galenic quality data is still useful for R06_qualities
The labels (645 unique words) may be substance names worth cross-referencing
The false positive teaches us: always do permutation tests, never trust raw chi-square
